meaning: adj.使恶化的,加重的,恼人的
examples: One cognitive theory suggests that aggravating and painful events trigger unpleasant feelings.
You don't realize how aggravating you can be.
This was an important factor aggravating the crisis.
Even so the mezzogiorno still has huge problems, which recession seems to be aggravating.
The jurors unanimously accepted eight of the 10 aggravating factors presented by the prosecution, Adams said.
